docs.title: Life of Caius Marius
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … of all the Romans the most upright and just, and maintained the honor of the consulate, without cringing or compliance, as strictly in accordance with ancient laws and usages, as though they had been immutable mathematical truths; and yet fell, I know not how, into some weaknesses,

docs.title: Life of Crassus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: But Crassus fared worse; day overtook him still deceived by Andromachus, and entangled in the fens and the difficult country. There were with him four cohorts of legionary soldiers, a very few horsemen, and five lictors, with whom having with
